mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on future earnings and supports risk taking; when the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is negative, the signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above recessionary levels signals weakening household income and earnings risk; when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, the signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: recession or early post-recession labor-market stress
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day returns after UNRATE crosses above 8.0% are positive in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth during elevated unemployment signals that consumption is stabilizing before labor markets heal, lifting near-term revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.5% and RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above 0%
regime_hint: post-shock consumer recovery with high unemployment
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than the unconditional sample average in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below its 2017 index base and still declining signals spare capacity and pressure on cyclical earnings; when INDPRO is below 100 and its 3-month change is negative, the signal predicts negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 100 and INDPRO 3-month change crosses below 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show median 20-day S&P 500 returns above zero and a negative-signal hit rate below 50% over the validation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a severe-stress threshold indicate weak construction activity, impaired credit demand, and reduced durable-goods spillovers; when HOUST crosses below 700, the signal predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following HOUST crossings below 700 are not lower than unconditional 60-day returns at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while policy rates remain near zero compresses real household purchasing power and threatens margins without a growth-confirming rate signal; this joint condition predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward return regressions and triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll momentum raises aggregate wage income and confirms improving business demand; higher 3-month PAYEMS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through rapid earnings-expectation adjustment.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month growth signal has a 5-day forward Rank IC below 0.02 in every rolling 2-year validation window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP and retail sales identifies broad demand contraction rather than isolated sector weakness; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth crosses below 0% and RSAFS 3-month growth crosses below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales contraction does not produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than either single-series contraction alone in walk-forward tests.
